gauge.name: Meramec River at Valley Park

impacts.statement: This represents the level at which, with no additional protection, the Valley Park levee would be overtopped at the upper end of the flood wall.
Near this height, the intersection of Big Bend Road and Dougherty Ferry Road begins flooding.
Interstate 44 is closed near this height.  During the December 2015 flood, this highway was closed from Bowles Avenue in Valley Park west to Missouri Route 109 exit in Eureka.
Near this height, Missouri Route 30 (Gravois Road) must be closed in both directions in St. Louis County.
Railroad bridge just downstream of the Highway 141 bridge begins to flood.
MODOT must take preventative action to keep I-44 lanes open west of Highway 141.
Water begins covering the BNSF rails at this height.
The final flood gate through the BNSF railroad path is closed at this height.

Old Highway 141 is closed at Gravois Road and at Gravois Bluffs Blvd.  This impact level may vary depending on the crest difference of the Mississippi River at St. Louis from 30.8 ft, the March 2008 crest.

In Fenton, the following roads are closed at this level:  all of Yarnell Road, Riverside Drive at Wolfner Drive, Fabricator Drive at Headland Drive, Soccer Park Road at the truck stop, Rudder Road at Fabick Drive, and Larkin Williams Road at Ferry Street.
The flood gate at Vance Road in Valley Park is closed at this height.

The flood gate at St. Louis Avenue in Valley Park is closed at this height.  Near this height, Vance Road begins to flood from just outside of the flood gate to Highland Village and Soccer Park Road from Cassens Drive to Yarnell Road begins to flood.
The northbound lanes of Highway 141 at I-44 will be closed near this height.
Near this height, Smizer Mill Road and Meramec Station Road begin flooding near their intersection.
The southbound lanes of Highway 141 at I-44 will be closed near this height.
Near this height, Yarnell Road north of the Soccer Park Road intersection begins to flood.  At this level, Rock Alva Drive (next to Tapawingo Golf Club) begins flooding.
Water reaches top of boat ramp at Valley Park Public Fishing Access.  Also, the Anheuser-Busch Soccer Park begins flooding near this height.

Yarnell Road is closed at Riverside Drive.  Larkin Williams is closed at Riverside Golf Club entrance.  In Valley Park...Tree Court Industrial Road begins flooding between Marshall Road and Scarlet Oak Boulevard.  Also...the golf course at Family Golf and Learning Center and the Kirkwood Athletic Association ball fields begin flooding near this height.

Water enters the SW corner of Larkin Williams and Yarnell Road intersection through a stormwater grate.  In Valley Park...Marshall Road begins to flood between Tree Court Industrial Road and Timberbrook Drive.
Buder Park and the recreation park at the water plant floods.
The south riverward side of Marshall Road begins to flood.
Sluice gates at 5th Street, 3rd Street, and Fishpot Creek at Union Pacific Railway tracks must be closed.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
